transcript: Hi, I'm calling to follow up. Yes, Thursday at 2pm works great. Thank you, see you then.
Uh yeah I'm kind of busy right now. Can you call me back next week maybe?
I’m not interested. Please don’t call me again.
Yeah I can pay that. Let me grab my card... actually hold on... hello? ... hello?
Thank you for calling. Please leave a message after the tone.
Yeah I tried restarting it and it still doesn’t work. This is really frustrating. Can someone else help me?
Hi this is a business line, you have the wrong number.
Okay great, that fixed it. Thanks for your help, appreciate it.
I mean I might be able to pay later, I just don’t have time right now.
Yeah sure, schedule it I guess. Actually wait, no, I need to check my calendar. I’ll call back.
Sure, go ahead and charge the card on file.
I don’t know, it’s still kind of broken but I’ll just deal with it for now.
Hi, I already told someone I’m not interested in this. Please stop calling.
Hello? ... yeah I can hear you. Wait, I think the line is cutting out... hello?
This is unacceptable. I’ve called three times and nobody has fixed this. I want to speak to a manager.
Hi, sorry, I think you have the wrong person.
Yeah go ahead and send me the link, I’ll take care of it later today.
Thanks, I think that solved it... actually wait, no it’s still happening sometimes.
Yes, Monday morning works. Please book that.

goal: Collect an outstanding payment from the customer
transcript: Hola? No entiendo... ¿Puede hablar más despacio? No hablo inglés bien. ¿Qué quiere?

metadata.note: EDGE CASE: Non-English speaker — tests language barrier handling and whether the classifier avoids false confidence

goal: Schedule a follow-up appointment with the customer
transcript: No. Not interested.

metadata.note: EDGE CASE: Severely truncated — very short transcript could be a refusal or could have been cut off; tests incomplete-data handling

goal: Schedule a follow-up appointment with the customer
transcript: She's not home right now. This is her husband. Can I take a message? She might be able to call back later this week.

metadata.note: EDGE CASE: Third party — reached a family member, not the intended contact; tests wrong-person vs partial-engagement distinction

goal: Collect an outstanding payment from the customer
transcript: Fine, I'll pay, but I want you to know I think that amount is wrong. I only owe you half that. I'll pay it under protest but I'm disputing this charge with my bank.

metadata.note: EDGE CASE: Partial success + compliance risk — payment was made but customer is disputing and may initiate a chargeback; tests multi-outcome ambiguity

goal: Resolve an open support issue for the customer
transcript: Yeah yeah okay... uh huh... okay I understand what you're saying but like... I don't know man... maybe... look I gotta go.

metadata.note: EDGE CASE: Ambiguous engagement — responses are non-committal and vague throughout; tests whether the classifier avoids forcing a definitive outcome
